posts.title: We Were Never Meant to Survive - Reimagined (Duet)
posts.excerpt: A collision between two truths—love and duty—where neither side is willing to give in.
posts.content:
This is where everything comes together.

Not to resolve—but to clash.

Both sides are fully present here. There’s no misunderstanding anymore, no confusion about what the other wants. They both know exactly what they’re fighting for, and that’s what makes it impossible to fix.

The structure of the song reflects that—back and forth, constantly pushing against each other, neither one backing down. It’s not about convincing the other person anymore. It’s about standing your ground, even if it means the world can’t hold together because of it.

That’s really what this song is:

Two people who still care about each other,
but want completely incompatible things.

And neither of them is wrong.
posts.lyrics:
[Intro - Ambient synths and distant strings, haunting soundscape]

[Female Verse - Angel]
I feel you in the static now, Between each fading star— Every second splits in two, No matter where you are. I hear you tearing through the walls, Of realities I'm holding— Every timeline that you break, Is another world I'm folding.

[Male Verse - Grissom]
You keep hiding in the light, Like it makes you clean— I've seen who you become, When you're just with me. Before the weight of worlds, Before the cosmic lie— I've seen the girl who chose my hand, And I won't let her die.

[Female Pre-Chorus - Angel]
I can't keep this from breaking—

[Male Pre-Chorus - Grissom]
I won't let you escape it—

[Female Pre-Chorus - Angel]
Every choice I make destroys something—

[Male Pre-Chorus - Grissom]
Then destroy the right things for once—

[Female Chorus - Angel]
We were never meant to survive—

[Male Chorus - Grissom]
In a world that chose a side—

[Female Chorus - Angel]
I chose to save them all—

[Male Chorus - Grissom]
I hunt the version of you that's gone—

[Female Chorus - Angel]
I keep holding the world's light—

[Male Chorus - Grissom]
I burn through every night for you—

[Duet Chorus - Both]
Just to prove that love still matters—
When the end of time starts!

[Female Verse - Angel]
Every time you say my name, A timeline dies— I see the girl I used to be, In your eyes. The one who didn't carry worlds, The one who only carried you— But she's gone, and I can't bring her back, No matter what you do.

[Male Verse - Grissom]
Let it burn if that's the price, To get you back in my life— What good is saving everything, If you're not mine? I don't want their gratitude, I don't want their praise— I just want the girl who loved me, Before she learned to save.

[Female Pre-Chorus - Angel]
I hold all their lives in my hands—

[Male Pre-Chorus - Grissom]
I carry only what's lost—

[Female Pre-Chorus - Angel]
Can't you see I'm trying to protect them—

[Male Pre-Chorus - Grissom]
Can't you see I'm trying to protect us!

[Female Chorus - Angel]
We were never meant to survive—

[Male Chorus - Grissom]
In a world that chose a side—

[Female Chorus - Angel]
I chose to save them all—

[Male Chorus - Grissom]
I hunt the version of you that's gone—

[Female Chorus - Angel]
I keep holding the world's light—

[Male Chorus - Grissom]
I burn through every night for you—

[Duet Chorus - Both]
We were never meant to survive—
But we still try!

[Female Bridge - Angel]
There has to be another way—

[Male Bridge - Grissom]
There was — and you walked away!

[Female Bridge - Angel]
I did it to save them all—

[Male Bridge - Grissom]
You did it to let me fall!

[Female Bridge - Angel]
Every world I hold together—

[Male Bridge - Grissom]
Is another world without us!

[Female Bridge - Angel]
I'm doing what I have to—

[Male Bridge - Grissom]
You're running from what you want to!

[Female Bridge - Angel]
I can't be selfish—

[Male Bridge - Grissom]
You can't be selfless forever!

[Female Bridge - Angel]
Someone has to stand—

[Male Bridge - Grissom]
Then stand with me!

[Female Final Chorus - Angel]
We were never meant to survive—

[Male Final Chorus - Grissom]
Then let it end with us—

[Female Final Chorus - Angel]
I'll hold this broken universe—

[Male Final Chorus - Grissom]
I'll turn it into dust—

[Duet Final Chorus - Both]
WE WERE NEVER MEANT TO SURVIVE,
BUT WE WERE MEANT TO LOVE!

[Female Final Chorus - Angel]
And that's why I have to let you go—

[Male Final Chorus - Grissom]
And that's why I'll never stop!

[Female Outro - Angel]
Don't make me choose again...

[Male Outro - Grissom]
You already did.

[Female Outro - Angel]
I'm sorry...

[Male Outro - Grissom]
I'm not.

[Female Outro - Angel]
Goodbye, Grissom.

[Male Outro - Grissom]
I'll find you in the next one.

[Duet Outro - Both]
We were never meant to survive...

[Silence]

archiveMeta.description: A convergence event where opposing truths collide, and the timeline can no longer resolve itself.

posts.title: The One You Used to Be - Reimagined
posts.excerpt: A quiet, obsessive look back at the one version of love that felt real—and the refusal to let it stay in the past.
posts.content:
This song lives in that space before everything breaks.

It’s not about what actually happened—it’s about what felt right, and how hard it is to let that version of someone go once you’ve seen it. There’s this idea running through it that somewhere, in at least one timeline, things worked. And if that’s true, then maybe it’s not impossible to get it back.

It’s the softest song emotionally, but also one of the most dangerous.
Because once you believe there was a perfect version of someone… it becomes really hard to accept anything else.
posts.lyrics:
[Intro - Ambient textures, distant guitar, heartbeat rhythm]

[Verse 1 - Intimate, aching]
I saw you in a broken sky,
Still saying that you had to try—
You looked at me like I was gone,
Like I was something you moved on from.

Like I was just a chapter,
In a story you outgrew—
But I'm still reading that same page,
The one where I still had you.

[Verse 2 - Building pain]
Every version of your face,
Feels like it's in the wrong place—
None of them look back at me,
Like you did when you were free.

Free from the weight of worlds,
Free from the cosmic debt—
Just a girl who loved a boy,
Before she learned regret.

[Pre-Chorus - Desperate longing]
There was a time you stayed,
There was a time you meant it—
There was a version of your heart,
That beat in time with mine.

And I need that back.
I need her back.

[Chorus - Emotional build, obsessive devotion]
I don't want who you are now,
I want the one you used to be—
Before the world got in your way,
Before you walked away from me.

I don't need a thousand lives,
I just need the right one—
Where you still look at me,
Like I'm the only one.

Like I matter more than stars.
Like I'm worth more than their lives.

[Verse 3 - Raw vulnerability]
I hear your voice in shattered days,
Telling me it's not the same—
But I've been through a hundred worlds,
And you were perfect in just one.

Perfect in the way you laughed,
Perfect in the way you stayed—
Perfect in how you chose my hand,
Before duty led you astray.

[Pre-Chorus 2 - More intense]
I won't forget that place,
I won't forget your eyes—
The way they looked at me that night,
Before you said goodbye.

Before you broke us both.
Before you became this.

[Chorus 2 - Peak emotion]
I don't want who you are now,
I want the one you used to be—
The one who never turned around,
The one who stayed with me.

I don't care what breaks or burns,
To bring that moment back—
I'll tear apart forever,
Just to get you back.

Just to see you smile at me,
Like you did before the world took you away.

[Bridge - Quiet desperation building to conviction]
They say I've lost my mind,
But I've just seen too much—
You only loved me once,
But that one time was enough.

Enough to make me cross realities,
Enough to make me destroy—
Enough to make me hunt through infinite futures,
For the girl who was my world.

And I'll find her.
I'll find you.
The real you.

[Final Chorus - Desperate, powerful]
I DON'T WANT WHO YOU ARE NOW,
I WANT THE ONE YOU USED TO BE—
THE GIRL WHO CHOSE MY NAME,
OVER ALL OF REALITY!

I DON'T NEED EVERY FUTURE,
I JUST NEED THAT ONE—
WHERE YOU WERE STILL IN LOVE WITH ME,
WHEN EVERYTHING BEGUN!

BEFORE THE FRACTURE.
BEFORE THE CHOICE.
BEFORE YOU LEARNED TO SAVE EVERYONE BUT US.

[Outro - Fading to whisper, haunted]
I remember you...
The way you were...
The way we were...

And I'll burn every false version,
Until I find the truth...

Until I find the Angel,
Who still loves me too.

I remember you.

[Final note sustained then silence]

archiveMeta.description: A preserved fragment from before the fracture - where love existed without cost.
archiveMeta.systemNote: Reference timeline detected. Emotional imprint preserved.

posts.title: You Were Better Before You Saved the World - Reimagined
posts.excerpt: An unhinged response to loss, where love turns into obsession and the world becomes something worth destroying.
posts.content:
This is what happens when someone refuses to accept the choice that was made.

It’s angry, but not in a simple way. It’s controlled at first, almost logical—like he genuinely believes he’s the one who’s right. That if there was a version of her that chose him, then that version is the one that matters.

From there, it spirals.

The idea of “fixing” things becomes more important than anything else, even if it means tearing everything apart to do it. It’s not just grief anymore—it’s determination, taken too far.

This song is the point where love stops being something shared… and becomes something taken.
posts.lyrics:
[Intro - Distorted guitars, industrial atmosphere, heavy drums building]

[Verse 1 - Controlled rage, bitter]
You say the sky is falling down,
Like it's something new—
I watched you choose a thousand lives
Over me and you.

Every time you close your eyes,
I see the girl you were—
Before you learned how to be God,
And forgot what love was worth.

I remember when you smiled at me,
Like I was your whole world—
Now you look at me like I'm just another
Timeline you had to burn.

[Pre-Chorus - Building intensity]
There was one reality,
Where you stayed with me—
And I swear it was the only one
That ever felt real.

The only one that mattered.
The only one worth saving.

[Chorus - Explosive, obsessive]
You were better before you saved the world,
Before you learned to let me burn—
Now you call this sacrifice,
But it feels like you just chose their lives
Over mine!

I remember you,
Do you remember me?
Or did you erase that too,
When you became their fucking deity?

[Verse 2 - Darker, more dangerous]
I've killed a thousand versions
Of the girl I used to know—
Every one of them was closer
To the one I lost.

They all look at me with your eyes,
And beg me to stop—
But I'm not killing you,
I'm fixing what you broke.

Every Angel I destroy,
Brings me closer to the truth—
That somewhere in this shattered mess,
There's still a you that chose me too.

[Pre-Chorus 2 - Obsessive conviction]
There's one true version of your heart,
And I know it's mine—
I'll burn every false reality,
Until I find it.

I'll tear through every lie you've built.
Every world you're holding.

[Chorus 2 - Peak rage and grief]
You were better before you saved the world,
Before your hope became a curse—
Now you hide behind the light,
While I drown inside your lies!

But I remember you,
When you still chose us—
When you looked at me and saw forever,
Not just cosmic dust.

[Instrumental Break - Aggressive, chaotic]

[Bridge - Building from quiet menace to explosion]
You say I'm wrong,
You say I'm insane—
But I've lived a hundred lives,
In a hundred different flames.

And in every single one
Where you don't leave,
You're finally happy...
With me.

So don't you dare tell me I'm the monster,
When you're the one who walked away—
Don't you dare call this obsession,
When you made me this way!

[Final Chorus - Maximum power, unhinged]
YOU WERE BETTER BEFORE YOU SAVED THE WORLD,
BEFORE YOU LET IT TAKE YOUR SOUL—
I DON'T NEED A UNIVERSE,
I JUST NEED THE GIRL I KNOW!

SO I'LL TEAR THIS ALL APART,
TILL I FIND YOUR TRUEST HEART—
I REMEMBER YOU,
YOU WERE MINE!

AND YOU'LL BE MINE AGAIN!

[Outro - Distorted, fading to whisper]
Come back to me...
In the right timeline...

I'll find you...
I'll find the real you...

The one who loved me back.

[Final distorted note cutting to silence]

archiveMeta.description: A hostile fragment where loss becomes obsession, and one version of him refuses to accept the world she chose.
archiveMeta.systemNote: Hostile recursion detected. Subject actively destabilizing timelines.

posts.title: Still Breathing (In a Dying World) - Reimagined
posts.excerpt: A turning point where choosing to save everything means losing the one thing that mattered most.
posts.content:
This is the moment where everything changes.

It’s about making a choice you know you can’t undo—and choosing it anyway. Not because it’s easy, but because it feels like the only thing you can live with. There’s a lot of restraint in this one. It’s not explosive, it’s controlled. Like someone holding everything together while it’s already falling apart.

The core idea is simple:
If saving everyone means losing the person you love, do you still do it?

She does.
And that decision is what breaks everything that comes after.
posts.lyrics:
[Intro - Ethereal synths, heartbeat rhythm, ambient soundscape]

[Verse 1 - Intimate, reverent]
The sky keeps tearing at the seams,
Like it's tired of holding us—
Every street is full of ghosts
That used to be our love.

I count the cracks in every star,
Like they're mistakes I made—
Every time I try to fix one,
Another fades away.

I'm standing in the wreckage now,
Of futures I let die—
To save the ones who'll never know
The price of being alive.

[Pre-Chorus - Building emotion]
I hear your voice in broken days,
You're calling from a life we lost—
But if I turn to look for you,
This whole world pays the cost.

And I can't let them fall,
Even if it means losing you—
Even if it means losing everything.

[Chorus - Soaring, aching]
I'm still breathing in a dying world,
Still holding onto what we were—
Every heartbeat breaks the light,
But I won't let it go tonight.

If this is how forever ends,
I'll stand here till the bitter end—
I'm still breathing,
I'm still breathing,
For us... for them... for all of it.

[Verse 2 - More desperate]
The ocean swallowed every road
That led me back to you—
I write your name in shattered glass,
Like something I still owe.

Every version of your eyes
Is begging me to stay—
But the future screams my name,
And pulls me away.

I see the girl I used to be,
In timelines turning dust—
The one who chose your hand,
Before she learned what love could cost.

[Pre-Chorus 2 - Raw vulnerability]
You say there's one reality
Where love is all we need—
But every time I try to reach it,
Another world just bleeds.

And I can't watch them burn,
Even if it means burning alone—
Even if you hate me for it.

[Chorus 2 - Peak power]
I'm still breathing in a dying world,
Still fighting what we can't reverse—
If I let myself fall back,
Everything we save will crack.

So I'll carry this alone,
With your shadow in my bones—
I'm still breathing,
I'm still breathing,
For us... for worlds you'll never see.

[Bridge - Stripped down, building dramatically]
If loving you means letting go,
Then carve my heart in stone—
I'd rather lose a thousand lives,
Than lose the ones I don't know.

I hear you in the silence now,
Like you never left—
But I can't burn down every world,
Just to save what we had.

I can't... I won't...
Even though it kills me.
Even though you'll never understand.

[Final Chorus - Maximum emotion]
I'M STILL BREATHING IN A DYING WORLD,
STILL PRAYING FOR A DIFFERENT TURN—
IF THERE'S A PLACE WHERE WE SURVIVE,
PROMISE ME YOU'LL KEEP ME ALIVE.

EVEN IF I NEVER SEE,
THE LIFE YOU'RE REMEMBERING—
I'M STILL BREATHING,
I'M STILL BREATHING,
FOR US!

[Outro - Fading, broken]
I'm still breathing...
For every world that needs me...
For every timeline I'm holding together...

I'm still breathing...
Even though you're gone...
Even though I chose this...

Don't forget me...
In the timeline where you stayed.

[Final ambient fade to silence]

archiveMeta.description: The moment she chose everything, knowing it would cost her the one thing she wanted to keep.
archiveMeta.systemNote: Critical divergence detected. Global stability prioritized over personal attachment.

posts.title: Shattered Trust (Reimagined)
posts.excerpt: A post-collapse anthem about carrying the weight of a broken world—and the betrayal that caused it.
posts.content:
This is the aftermath.

By the time this song happens, the damage is already done. The trust is gone, the timelines are breaking, and there’s no version of things where it all works out cleanly anymore. But instead of giving up, she keeps going anyway.

That’s what this song is really about—endurance.

Not hope in the usual sense, but the kind where you keep holding everything together even when you know it’s costing you more than you can afford. There’s anger in it, but it’s quieter than you’d expect. It’s less about lashing out, and more about accepting what something cost you and choosing to carry it anyway.
posts.lyrics:
[Intro - Ambient synths with fractured glass sounds, soft piano]

[Verse 1 - Ethereal, vulnerable]
The sky is cracked above my head,
The world I held is bleeding red—
I trusted someone I should not,
And now the timeline's all but lost.

Every star I tried to save,
Every promise that I gave—
Turned to ash between my hands,
While reality collapses where I stand.

[Verse 2 - Building intensity]
Every choice I made in vain,
Every life I tried to save—
Betrayal cuts deeper than the night,
Still I must stand, still I must fight.

I see the fractures spreading wide,
See the worlds I've left behind—
Each one screaming out my name,
Each one dying just the same.

[Pre-Chorus - Voice cracking with emotion]
I won't collapse, I won't fall,
Even if I bear it all—
Even if the weight might break me,
I'll hold the pieces of what's left.

[Chorus - Dramatic swell, powerful but aching]
I'm walking through the shards of us,
I'm holding on, though broken and crushed—
I trusted you, it's true,
But I will carry all we lose.

Every fragment cuts my hands,
Every memory turns to sand—
But I'll keep the worlds from dying,
Even while I'm slowly breaking inside.

[Interlude - Piano and strings, mournful]

[Verse 3 - Raw delivery]
Your shadow lingers in my mind,
I see the past we left behind—
The version of you that I believed,
Was just another lie reality conceived.

I gave my heart to something gone,
Built my hope on what was wrong—
But still I move forward, still I'm strong,
Even though I knew the truth all along.

[Pre-Chorus 2 - More desperate]
I feel the sting, I feel the pain,
But hope remains beneath the strain—
Or maybe that's just what I tell myself,
To keep from falling into the void.

[Chorus 2 - Peak emotion, building]
I'm walking through the shards of us,
I'm holding on, though broken and crushed—
I trusted you, it's true,
Now I carry all we lose.

Every timeline that I save,
Is another piece of you I gave away—
But I'll keep the light from fading,
Even if it costs me everything.

[Bridge - Stripped to piano, building to orchestral power]
Even if the world betrays me,
Even if it falls apart—
I'll stand within the ruins,
With courage in my heart.

Even if you never loved me,
Even if it was a lie—
I'll hold these fractured realities,
Till the day I die.

Because someone has to stand here,
Someone has to pay the cost—
And if that someone has to be me,
Then let me bear what's lost.

[Final Chorus - Maximum power but still aching]
I'M WALKING THROUGH THE SHARDS OF US,
I'M HOLDING ON, THOUGH BROKEN AND CRUSHED—
I TRUSTED YOU, IT'S TRUE,
BUT I WILL CARRY ALL WE LOSE!

EVERY WORLD THAT STILL SURVIVES,
IS BUILT ON PIECES OF MY LIFE—
BUT I'LL KEEP THE STARS FROM FALLING,
EVEN THOUGH I'M BARELY BREATHING!

[Outro - Fading to whisper, ambient synths]
Still I fight...
For worlds you'll never see...
For lives you'll never know...

Still I fight...
Even though you broke me...
Even though I'm all alone...

Still I fight.

[Final note - Single piano key, sustained then silence]

archiveMeta.description: A post-collapse fragment where trust failed, and the cost of saving everything became permanent.
archiveMeta.systemNote: Collapse event stabilized through force of will. Structural integrity compromised.

posts.title: "Hope's Song"
posts.excerpt: An uplifting anime-opening-style track about pushing through loneliness, finding your strength, and choosing to keep moving forward no matter what.
posts.content:
Hope's Song is one of those tracks that came from a really simple idea: what if moving forward is enough?

I wanted it to feel like an anime opening, energetic, emotional, and full of motion, but grounded in something real. The kind of song that starts in isolation and slowly builds into something stronger, not because everything gets better, but because you decide to keep going anyway.

The lyrics lean into that feeling of being closed off for a long time, unsure if hope is still meant for you, and then slowly reaching back toward it. Not perfectly, not confidently, just honestly.

The chorus is where that shift happens. It's not about becoming fearless, it's about accepting the scars and still choosing to chase something better. That idea carries through the whole song: staying true to yourself, even when it's hard, and trusting that there's still something ahead worth reaching for.

It's not a song about having everything figured out.
It's about deciding to move forward anyway.
posts.lyrics:
[VERSE 1]

Under the cloudy sky,
I wandered on my own for so long.
That little voice inside
Could never seem to reach anyone.

Behind the world I closed,
I wondered if the light still survived.
I held my weakness close,
But still I wanted to keep moving on.

[PRE-CHORUS]

If I reach out my hand
Past the tears I've cried,
There's a future somewhere
Calling me to try.

[CHORUS]

Now I'm running forward,
Following my heart-
Even all the broken days
Can turn into strength.

It's okay to be scarred,
As long as I still chase all my dreams.
I'll stay true to myself
And reach for the light I see.

[VERSE 2]

Every small hope I held
Begins to fill my chest with a fire.
Those trembling lonely nights
Were never wasted moments at all.

I push the door once closed,
And feel the gentle wind on my back.
Toward the dream long ago,
I take a step and I'm not turning back.

[PRE-CHORUS]

Even darkness around
Can become my guide,
If I learn to embrace
Who I am inside.

[CHORUS]

Now I'm rising higher,
Breaking through the pain-
Every heartbeat in my chest
Is lighting the way.

It's okay if I cry,
As long as I keep moving ahead.
I'll stay true to myself
And trust in the road I tread.

[BRIDGE]

Even when I lose my way again,
This small wish inside won't disappear.
I will stand and rise a thousand times,
Shouting out the truth- "I want to live!"

[FINAL CHORUS]

Now I'm running forward
Toward the endless sky-
Even when it hurts so much,
I know I'll survive.

Any darkness I face
Can brighten if I don't give it up.
I'll stay true to myself
And fly to the future I love.

collections.title: The Fractureverse
collections.description:
A fractured reality where every choice creates a new world, and every version of love carries a different cost.

Here, memory, sacrifice, collapse, obsession, and convergence exist side by side - none of them fully gone, none of them fully resolved.

collections.title: Main Verse
collections.description: The core releases that feel closest to the heart of the site: personal, direct, and emotionally central.

collections.title: Anime Openings
collections.description: Songs with big movement, bright hooks, and that opening-sequence sense of forward motion.

collections.title: Healing Arc
collections.description: Tracks about recovery, resilience, and choosing to keep going while the picture is still incomplete.

about.heroTitle: I make music to understand things I don’t know how to say out loud.
about.heroText:
Most of what I create starts as a feeling I can’t quite explain. 
Songs are how I process it, and this site is where those pieces stay connected — 
not just the music, but everything around it.
about.artistTitle: I follow emotion first, and figure out meaning after.
about.artistText:
I tend to build songs from atmosphere, imagery, and whatever feeling won’t leave me alone. 
A lot of what I make leans into cinematic emotion and narrative structure — not always with a clear story, 
but with something that feels like it could be one.

I’m less interested in perfect answers and more interested in capturing something honest, 
even if it’s unfinished or complicated.
about.siteTitle: This is where everything comes together.
about.siteText:
I didn’t want my music to exist as isolated posts or uploads. 
Each release here has space to breathe — with notes, context, and connections to other pieces.

Collections let me group songs into larger ideas, and pages like the Fractureverse turn them into something closer to a world. 
It’s less about organizing content, and more about keeping meaning intact.
about.quoteTitle: Some feelings don’t go away until you turn them into something.
about.quoteText: Some feelings don’t go away until you turn them into something.
